title: Law Fellow
description_text: Senator Adam Schiff (D-CA), a member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, seeks a post-graduate Legal Fellow for a one-year term. Start date is flexible. Applicants must be a recent law school graduate with the ability to secure fellowship funding from their law school or another source. The Office will coordinate as necessary for external funding opportunities identified by applicants. The Legal Fellow will join Senator Schiff's Senate Judiciary Committee team and engage in a range of activities, including vetting and advising on nominations, oversight investigations, conducting legal and policy research, supporting preparation for committee hearings, and supporting policy and legislation development. The Fellow also may collaborate with staff members on policy issues outside the jurisdiction of the Judiciary Committee. Candidates must have strong writing and research skills, experience working in a fast-paced environment, and thrive in a collaborative team. California ties are preferred, but not required. Senator Schiff’s office is an equal opportunity employer. People of color, women, LGBTQ+ and veterans are strongly encouraged to apply. The office does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, military status, gender identity and expression, marital status, parental status, or sexual orientation. Please apply by March 20, using our online portal: Law Fellow – Washington, DC . Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. Please no unsolicited drop-ins, phone calls, or emails to the office.

title: Auditor
description_text: The Committee on Rules and Administration seeks an auditor to join the non-designated team.  Responsibilities include but are not limited to verifying vouchers and completing supporting documentation to ensure that all information is accurate and payment requirements are met; posting vouchers and approving certifications or rejecting incorrect vouchers in accordance with laws and regulations; engaging with Senate offices to clarify and collect necessary information. Auditor will provide necessary assistance to Administrative Managers, Chief Clerks, and other staff from Senate offices regarding questions on statutes, rules, travel regulations, and financial questions regarding official funds and processing waivers of regulations. Additionally, this person will provide support to Majority, Minority, and non-designated staff upon request.  Ideal candidates will have strong interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills, a bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, or business-related field, and 1-3 years of experience in accounts payable/auditing with proficiency in auditing principles. Strong ability to analyze financial data to ensure thorough audit of complex vouchers is a must. Senate experience desired, but not required. Salary commensurate with experience.

title: Information Technology Deputy Director
description_text: A Senate office seeks a highly experienced Information Technology (IT) Deputy Director to assist in supporting and maintaining IT infrastructure, encompassing a range of technologies such as SharePoint, Exchange, Active Directory, VMware, SANs, host servers, etc.  The role demands a skilled professional adept in managing both the hardware and software aspects of a complex IT environment in a fast-paced and team-focused environment. Key Responsibilities include the following: Network and Server Management: Administer and support server environments, including but not limited to VMware, SANs, and host servers. Configure and maintain network hardware (routers, switches, firewalls) and software. Application Management: Manage and support enterprise applications such as SharePoint and Exchange. Oversee Active Directory for user and computer account management, group policies, and authentication services. End-User and Mobile Device Support: Provide comprehensive technical support, including mobile devices and office productivity software. Educate staff on best practices for utilizing enterprise applications and IT resources. Security and Compliance: Implement network security measures and maintain compliance standards. Regularly update security protocols and perform network audits. Disaster Recovery and Data Backup: Develop and manage disaster recovery plans, especially concerning virtualized environments and data storage solutions. Technological Innovation Proficiency: Maintain proficiency in current and emerging technological advancements. Ability to multitask and ready to tackle challenges in a dynamic IT environment. Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related degree. Relevant certifications (CompTIA A+, Network+, CCNA, MCSE, VMware Certified Professional). Extensive experience in managing and supporting SharePoint, Exchange, Active Directory, VMware, SANs, and host servers. Proficiency in mobile device management, including iOS and Android platforms. Strong knowledge of network infrastructure, server configurations, and data storage solutions. Excellent troubleshooting and problem-solving skills. Demonstrated record in horizon-scanning, anticipating problems, and researching cutting edge solutions. Top Secret / Sensitive Compartment Information (TS/SCI) security clearance required. Applicants also must be able to pass a background investigation. Working Environment: Standard office hours with a requirement for occasional after-hours support. Physical capability to manage server room equipment and perform hardware maintenance. Ability to travel to offsite locations occasionally.

title: Senate Resume Bank
description_text: The Senate Employment Office is accepting submissions for the 2026 Senate Resume Bank. Individuals interested in entry-level through professional staff opportunities with a Senator or Senate Committee are invited to complete a profile. Registrants will remain eligible for referral from January 1 through March 31, 2026. Please visit the Senate Employment Office’s website to learn more and register: https://employment.senate.gov/resume-bank/.

title: Legal Counsel
description_text: Senate Republican      Committee Staff is seeking a Legal Counsel. The ideal candidate must have      3-5 years of relevant experience to be considered. Candidates must have      strong written communication and analytical skills and be able to work      under tight deadlines. Successful candidates will be responsible for      conducting oversight of federal agencies, advising on policy matters, and      executing legal tasks pertaining to congressional oversight. Specific      issue areas can be shaped by the interest and experience of the successful      candidate and existing staff. This is NOT an entry level position. Prior      Capitol Hill experience is strongly preferred. Interested      candidates should send their resume to gopsenatorjob@gmail.com with      the subject line “Counsel Opening”.

title: Summer 2026 Regional Internship
description_text: U.S. Senator Kirsten E. Gillibrand (D-NY) is currently accepting applications for her Summer 2026 Regional Internship program for the upcoming semester. Responsibilities will include, but not be limited to constituent service work and a variety of administrative duties. All applicants should be responsible self-starters who possesses strong writing, research, and interpersonal skills. We encourage current and recent undergraduate students with a background in political science and international relations to apply, but this is not required. New York ties are preferred. Internships are available in Senator Gillibrand’s state offices, which are located in: Albany, Buffalo, Hudson Valley (Yonkers), Long Island (Melville), New York City, Rochester, and Syracuse. You will have the opportunity to indicate your office preferences when you complete the online application. This internship will run from early January to mid-May. Interested candidates can apply here: https://oampluspublic.senate.gov/public/login/4d627ec2-62a5-4b2d-95ff-87dce11b3e8a

title: Counsel
description_text: Conservative Republican Chairman seeking experienced oversight and investigations counsel for subcommittee. Responsibilities to include managing oversight and investigations projects, drafting letters and reports, conducting interviews, and other duties as needed to support the subcommittee’s work. Qualified candidates must be self-starters, possess exceptional written and oral communication skills, as well as time management skills, and a desire to work as part of a team. Two or more years of prior Hill or investigations experience preferred. Interested candidates should submit a resume.

title: Professional Staff Member
description_text: Senate Republican Committee Staff is seeking Professional Staff Members. The ideal candidates must have 3-5 years of relevant policy experience to be considered for a PSM position. Candidates must have strong written communication skills, attention to detail and be able to work under tight deadlines. Successful candidates will be responsible for conducting oversight of federal agencies, advising on policy matters, and managing legislation within their assigned issue portfolio. Issue portfolios can be shaped by the interest and experience of successful candidates and existing staff. This is NOT an entry level position. Interested candidates should send their resume to gopsenatorjob@gmail.com with the subject line “PSM Opening”.

title: 2026-2027 Legal Fellow
description_text: U.S. Senator Jon Ossoff (D-GA) seeks a lawyer or recent law school graduate for a full-time Legal Fellowship, to last up to one year, beginning in August 2026. The Fellow will support the Senator’s policy work on issues in the legal team’s portfolio, including criminal justice, gun control, reproductive rights, voting rights, privacy, prison oversight, law enforcement, antitrust, bankruptcy, and judicial and Department of Justice nominations. Primary responsibilities include legal research, drafting legal memoranda and briefs, writing and editing oversight letters, meeting with and managing constituent relationships, advising the Senator on issues in the legal team’s portfolio, and other duties as assigned. The Fellow will work closely with and report to the Senator’s counsels. Candidates must be exceptionally strong writers, detail-oriented, self-starters, and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment. Prior experience in legislative and investigative work, such as working for an oversight committee or as a prosecutor, is a plus. Georgia ties preferred but not required. Candidates must possess a J.D. Fellows should plan to be based in or near Washington, D.C. This position is not paid, but the Office can coordinate with fellowship or external funding opportunities identified by potential Fellows. Interested parties should e-mail a cover letter, resume, writing sample of no more than 10 pages, and three professional references to jobs@ossoff.senate.gov by EOD April 1, 2026, indicating “2026-2027 Legal Fellow – ” followed by your name in the subject line. Incomplete and late applications will not be considered. No phone calls or drop-bys, please. The Office is an equal employment opportunity employer and candidates of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. The office does not discriminate on the bases of race, color, religion, national origin, disability, age, uniformed service, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or gender expression. Applicants with disabilities who require reasonable accommodation to participate effectively in the application or hiring processes are encouraged to request an accommodation at any time during those processes. Requests for accommodation should be directed to jobs@ossoff.senate.gov .

source_organization: Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions - Republicans
title: Majority HELP Committee Internship
description_text: The Senate Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions (HELP), chaired by Senator Cassidy (R-LA), offers an internship program for students and recent graduates interested in the legislative process and federal policymaking. The Committee has jurisdiction over issues including public health and biomedical research; food and drug safety; early childhood, K–12, and higher education; workforce development; labor and employment policy; workplace safety; and retirement and pension security. Applicants should indicate which policy areas interest them most. HELP Committee internships are based in Washington, D.C., and provide interns with the opportunity to work closely with professional staff to support the Committee’s legislative and administrative functions. Responsibilities may include conducting policy and legislative research, assisting with hearing and markup preparation, attending committee hearings and briefings, drafting memos and summaries, and providing general administrative and operational support. Application: https://www.help.senate.gov/internship-application Application will close on March 16, 2026.

title: Senate Sergeant at Arms Talent Network 2026
description_text: The Senate Sergeant at Arms Talent Network is a resume bank that helps us match qualified job seekers to current and future job openings. By submitting your resume, you agree to make your resume visible to our recruiting team. Our hiring team will review resumes to find candidates who may be suitable for new job postings. Should your resume match qualifications for specific vacancies, our hiring team may contact you directly to conduct a phone screening and/or ask you to submit an official application for a position. You must continue to apply separately to positions on the career site that are of interest to you. Your profile will remain in the Talent Network for the remainder of this calendar year. At the end of December each year, the resume bank closes and a new resume bank is created for the next year. You will receive an email notification when the resume bank closes to remind you to submit your resume to the new resume bank if you are still interested in job opportunities within the Senate Sergeant at Arms. Do not submit application materials or resumes via email. Emailed documents will not be accepted. If you wish to remove your resume from consideration at any point, simply log on to your account and withdraw your application.

title: Regional Director - Southern Arizona
description_text: The Office of U.S. Senator Ruben Gallego is seeking a community-driven Southern Arizona Director to lead the Senator’s engagement across the region. Reporting to the State Director, this role serves as the Senator’s primary representative and advisor on Southern Arizona issues, relationships, and regional priorities. The ideal candidate brings strong regional knowledge, established relationships with local elected officials and stakeholders, and the ability to represent the Senator independently in dynamic and politically sensitive settings. Key Responsibilities: Advise the Senator on Southern Arizona issues and regional political dynamics. Represent and accompany the Senator at meetings, community events, and town halls. Build and maintain strong relationships with local elected officials, tribal leaders, and community stakeholders. Coordinate with State and DC staff to elevate regional priorities. Collaborate with Scheduling and Communications teams on regional outreach and messaging. Preferred Qualifications: Deep familiarity with Southern Arizona communities and political landscape. Experience in community engagement, public affairs, or political organizing. Strong communication and public-speaking skills. Flexibility to work evenings/weekends and travel throughout Southern Arizona. To Apply: Submit a resume and three references to GallegoSenateResumes@gmail.com with the subject line: “Southern Arizona Director Application – [Your Name].” Applications reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.

title: Communications Internship
description_text: Senator Patty Murray is accepting applications for a paid, full-time communications internship for the summer semester. Communications interns work closely with the communications team and help compile daily press clips, closely follow the news, draft press releases and other materials, and work on a variety of other projects. Strong candidates are self-motivated, pay close attention to detail, have strong writing skills, and thrive in a fast-paced environment. Undergraduate students and recent graduates are invited to apply. State ties are a plus, and the internship will start at the end of May and end in August. The application deadline is Sunday, March 15th, 2026.

title: Delaware Legislative Internship
description_text: The Office of Senator Lisa Blunt Rochester (D-Del.) seeks motivated, organized, and hardworking college students and recent graduates committed to public service to serve as interns in our Delaware offices this Summer. The Summer internship term begins in June 2026 and ends in August 2026. Interns in the Senator’s offices in Delaware work closely with staff throughout the State to develop a thorough knowledge of local issues and constituent needs. Interns support office staff and answer constituent calls, conduct research, and monitor local news outlets for daily developments. State interns have the opportunity to attend events with Senator Blunt Rochester, such as constituent events and town hall meetings. Successful candidates will possess excellent written and oral communication skills, willingness to contribute to a team environment, and eagerness to learn about the political process and congressional affairs. Delaware ties are preferred. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, sexual orientation, or gender identity. Applications for Summer 2026 internships are due on Friday, March 13th at 11:59 PM. No phone calls, emails, or drop-ins please. Delaware Application Link - https://oampluspublic.senate.gov/public/login/72321b57-189b-4ff7-ab05-f41d877a2936

title: Legislative Internship
description_text: The Office of Senator Bernard Sanders is currently seeking paid legislative interns for the Summer 2026 class. Legislative internships are geared toward Vermonters and other entry-level candidates. Legislative interns in the Senator’s Washington office answer phones and process voicemails, greet front desk visitors, sort mail and scans, attend hearings and briefings and take notes for staff, lead tours of the Capitol, provide additional research capacity to legislative staff, draft legislative mail, provide assistance to senior staff like Administrative Director and Chief of Staff, conduct rapid response, run errands in the Capitol complex, help with setup for events, and assist with staff projects as needed. This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, age, religion, disability, national origin, genetic information, uniformed status, sexual orientation, or gender identity or expression. People of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, working class individuals, and those from other traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Interested applicants may apply here: https://www.sanders.senate.gov/vermont/services/internships/ . The deadline to apply is Sunday, March 15.

title: Press and Video Internship
description_text: The Office of Senator Bernard Sanders is currently seeking paid press and video interns for the Summer 2026 class. Press and video interns in the Senator’s Washington D.C. office will need to have interest in learning Adobe Premiere Pro and the Adobe Suite, as well as a drive to work hard, pay close attention to detail, and explore creative approaches to video storytelling. Interns may be expected to assist with video shoots, create graphics, source interviewees, research archival footage, photos, public records, edit videos and create motion design and animation. This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, age, religion, disability, national origin, genetic information, uniformed status, sexual orientation, or gender identity or expression. People of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, working class individuals, and those from other traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Interested applicants may apply here: https://www.sanders.senate.gov/vermont/services/internships/ . The deadline to apply is Sunday, March 15.

title: State Office Internship
description_text: The Office of Senator Bernard Sanders is currently seeking paid state office interns for the Summer 2026 class. Interns in the Vermont office experience the various ways in which Senator Sanders’ staff assists Vermonters on a day-to-day basis, from representing the Senator at events to helping Vermonters resolve problems with federal agencies. Interns will help respond to constituent requests for information and assistance; join visits with Vermont organizations; help organize and staff public meetings and events, track local news stories, and much more. This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, age, religion, disability, national origin, genetic information, uniformed status, sexual orientation, or gender identity or expression. People of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, working class individuals, and those from other traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Interested applicants may apply here: https://www.sanders.senate.gov/vermont/services/internships/ . The deadline to apply is Sunday, March 15.

source_organization: Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs - Republicans
title: Summer 2026 Internship – Senate Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs (Majority Staff)
description_text: The Majority Staff for the Senate Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs Committee is seeking full- or part-time interns for the summer. Interns will gain firsthand experience with the legislative process and the Committee’s work on financial services, housing, banking, capital markets, and economic policy. Responsibilities include processing constituent correspondence, tracking legislation and policy developments, observing hearings, briefings, and markups, and conducting legislative and policy research. Candidates should have strong research and writing skills and be able to work in a fast-paced environment. Previous Hill experience or South Carolina ties are strongly preferred, but not required. This is a paid internship, and applicants should be available from May until August. Please submit a resume and cover letter. The application deadline is March 20, 2026.

title: Field Representative
description_text: Senator Martin Heinrich (D-NM) seeks a Field Representative to work on issues related to public lands, agriculture, environment, natural disasters, and military affairs. The position is based in Las Cruces, but includes travel throughout surrounding counties. Primary duties include identifying and accomplishing community initiatives with an emphasis on assigned issue areas; acting as the Senator’s representative within assigned issue areas; planning public meetings and events and staffing the Senator in the region; proactively building relationships with stakeholders, staying up-to-date on relevant news, and frequently interacting with constituents, community leaders, and organizations; working with the Senator’s state, legislative and economic development staff to recommend and implement solutions to state and regional challenges with a federal nexus and to bring increased federal funding to the region. The ideal candidate has strong ties to New Mexico, a history working with local stakeholders, is an enthusiastic team player with creative drive and overall exceptional writing and communications skills. This is not an entry level position; three to five years of applicable work experience are strongly preferred. Fluency in Spanish is a significant plus. This position is required to work in the office. Our team is driven by a desire to make a difference for our constituents. We are relentless in our work to create change and deliver for the people of New Mexico – and we have fun doing it. Interested parties should apply using this link: https://oampluspublic.senate.gov/public/login/70975d60-52fe-4c8d-9294-73c3ab361590 . This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, age, religion, disability, national origin, uniformed status, sexual orientation, or gender identity or expression. Application materials must be received by 5:00 pm MT on Friday, March 13, 2026. Applications received early will be given preference.

title: Videography Assistant
description_text: The office of Senator Kirsten Gillibrand is looking for a Videography Assistant to take our digital storytelling to the next level. This role is perfect for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment at the intersection of politics, media, and film. The ideal candidate has at least 1 year of relevant experience and thrives in a fast-paced environment. Key Responsibilities: Create compelling video: Use video to communicate the Senator's work in a compelling way to the American public. Produce end-to-end: Concept, shoot, edit, and deliver content on quick turnarounds. Experiment & innovate: Use new formats, vertical video, captions, graphics, and trends to meet audiences where they are. Stay agile: Capture both polished productions and scrappy real-time moments, from press conferences to committee hearings. Preferred Qualifications: 1 year of experience in video production, preferably in politics, advocacy, journalism, or creative media. A strong portfolio that shows you can tell stories that break through online. Expertise in Adobe Creative Suite (Premiere Pro, After Effects, Photoshop, Audition). Skilled at shooting on DSLR/mirrorless cameras, mobile, and in-the-moment environments. A deep understanding of digital trends, audience engagement, and what makes compelling video. Comfortable juggling multiple deadlines in a fast-paced environment. Bonus Points If You Have: Experience in livestreaming or producing broadcast-quality video. Skills in animation, motion graphics, or graphic design are a big plus. Basic familiarity with Congress, federal policy, or political campaigns. Interested candidates should submit a resume and a cover letter that includes a link to a digital portfolio containing examples of photography, graphic design, and/or video production.

title: Press Secretary
description_text: The Press Secretary will lead national media strategy and bookings for a Republican U.S. Senator, securing high-profile appearances across local and major television networks, radio, and print media. This role requires a skilled political communicator with strong relationships in national media and the ability to drive coverage in fast-moving news environments. Key Responsibilities Secure      and manage national TV, radio, and print bookings Pitch      stories, interviews, and op-eds to major outlets Develop      proactive media strategy and handle rapid response Prepare      the Senator for interviews with briefing materials Draft      press statements and monitor daily news coverage Qualifications Proven      track record of national bookings Strong      relationships with producers and reporters Excellent      writing and messaging skills

title: Legislative Director
description_text: Conservative Republican Senator is seeking candidates for the Legislative Director position in the office. This position serves as the Senator’s top policy advisor and manages the office’s legislative team and strategy. This is a senior staff position for a strategic thinker with a deep understanding of the Senate legislative process and the ability to work closely with the administration, leadership, and committee staff. The ideal candidate will be a strong manager, organized, and a trusted advisor who can translate the Senator’s priorities into effective legislative outcomes. Key Responsibilities: Manage and oversee the legislative team, setting      priorities and ensuring coordination across the portfolios Serve as the Senator’s principal legislative and      policy advisor Develop and execute the Senator’s legislative strategy      in coordination with leadership, committees, and the Administration Track floor activity, hearings, and negotiations to      ensure the office is positioned proactively Ensure the legislative team remains organized,      disciplined, and aligned with the Senator’s goals Provide strategic guidance on policy development,      messaging, and stakeholder engagement Qualifications: Strong understanding of the Senate legislative process Experience working with or alongside the      Administration Proven ability to manage staff and drive results under      pressure Excellent judgment, communication skills, and      attention to detail Prior Senate experience is not required but highly      recommended. Interested candidates should email resumes to senateld2026@gmail.com with Legislative Director in the subject line.

title: Paid Summer 2026 Press Intern – Senator Tina Smith
description_text: The Office of U.S. Senator Tina Smith is accepting applications for a summer press intern in her Washington, DC office. This is a paid position. Press intern s assist the communications team with compiling daily news clips, developing social media content, helping prepare online content, and other projects. Intern s will be selected based on the quality of their application and demonstrated commitment to public service. College students and recent college graduates are eligible to apply. Minnesota ties are preferred. The Senator’s office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of an individual’s race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, or disability. The office encourages individuals from all backgrounds to apply. Interested candidates should submit their resume, cover letter (indicating weekly availability), and three references (name and contact information including email and phone numbers) in a single pdf file to intern s@smith.senate.gov by March 15th. Please format the subject line of your email "First Name Last Name – Press – Summer 2026."

title: Paid Summer 2026 Legislative Intern – Senator Tina Smith
description_text: The Office of U.S. Senator Tina Smith is accepting applications for summer legislative intern s in her Washington, DC office. This is a paid position. Legislative intern s are paired with a legislative staff member who works on the intern's policy issue of interest and will assist this staffer with policy research as well as attending committee hearings and other Senate events. Intern s will be selected based on the quality of their application and demonstrated commitment to public service. College students and recent college graduates are eligible to apply. Minnesota ties are preferred. The Senator’s office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of an individual’s race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, or disability. The office encourages individuals from all backgrounds to apply. Interested candidates should submit their resume, cover letter (indicating weekly availability), and three references (name and contact information including email and phone numbers) in a single pdf file to intern s@smith.senate.gov by March 15th. Please format the subject line of your email "First Name Last Name – Legislative – Summer 2026.”

title: Digital Aide
description_text: Progressive Democratic Senator seeks a Digital Aide who can immediately contribute to a very fast-paced communications team. Ideal candidate has advanced editing skills in vertical video, proficiency in graphic animation editing, and advanced knowledge of major social media platform requirements for video. Responsibilities include producing high-quality graphics and editing videos for social media platforms; drafting written content such as copy, quotes, and video scripts; transcribing interviews; rapid response clipping of video interviews; and supporting other members of the communications team. This is a junior position with flexibility on years of experience, but requires technical skills in video editing and production, content capture, and graphic design. Key skills include advanced knowledge of Adobe Premiere Pro and After Effects, and proficiency with Adobe Creative Suite. The Senator’s office is an equal opportunity employer. The office does not discriminate on the basis of an individual’s race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, or uniformed service. The office is committed to inclusion and encourages all individuals from all backgrounds to apply. Please submit a resume and cover letter to DemDigitalAide@gmail.com by Monday, February 23th.

title: Executive Assistant
description_text: Senior Democratic Senator seeks an Executive Assistant to serve in Washington, DC office. Candidates should possess a meticulous attention to detail, demonstrate excellent communications skills, and have the ability to prioritize and follow-through on multiple tasks in a high pressure/fast-paced environment. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to: managing the Senator’s calls, correspondence, and records, ensuring the Senator has necessary briefing materials, organizing incoming scheduling requests, and supporting and working collaboratively with Schedulers to facilitate efficient executive operations. This role does not involve driving. The ideal candidate will exercise discretion and confidentiality, have a strong work ethic, and be available to work flexible hours including evenings/weekends when necessary. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, age, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability. Interested applicants should send a cover letter, resume, and references to schedulingjobs@email.senate.gov with Executive Assistant job in the subject line.

title: Legislative Correspondent
description_text: Northeast Democratic Senator seeks Legislative Correspondent to focus on defense and foreign affairs portfolio. Candidate must have or be able to obtain a TS-SCI clearance. Primary responsibilities include responding to constituent correspondence, attending constituent meetings and conducting research.  This position requires excellent communication, research and writing skills, as well as the ability to work well under pressure and in a fast-paced environment.  Hill experience and Northeast ties a plus.  This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, age, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability. Interested applicants should email a cover letter and resume to legjobs@email.senate.gov , ATTN: Defense and Foreign Affairs Legislative Correspondent in the subject line.

title: Economist
description_text: Liberty      Minded Republican Senator seeks an Economist. Qualified candidates should      have knowledge of budgetary and economic issues as well as a general      breadth of knowledge on the Senator’s priorities and Senate      procedure. Prior Senate or House experience writing Congressional      budget resolutions is strongly preferred.  Must be a strong writer,      researcher, and display the ability to think critically and outside the      box. Responsibilities include staffing and briefing the Senator on      relevant topics and drafting amendments, legislation, and budgetary      proposals. This is not an entry level position. Qualified      candidates should email a resume and cover letter to gopsenatorjob@gmail.com with      the Subject Line “Economist Opening”.

title: Communications Assistant
description_text: Democratic Senator is seeking a full-time, in-person Communications Assistant to join a fast-paced communications operation in Washington, D.C. Responsibilities for this role include compiling daily news clips, maintaining press contact lists, and helping oversee the press internship program, as well as drafting traditional (releases, media advisories, etc.) and digital (social media posts and videos, metrics tracking, etc.) materials. Candidates who write well, think creatively, work well on a team, and have impeccable attention to detail and a willingness to learn are encouraged to apply. Spanish language and video editing skills are a plus. The Office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, disability, age, uniformed service, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression. This is an entry-level position. Please submit one PDF with your resume, cover letter, and two short (one or two page) writing samples. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

title: Legislative Assistant
description_text: Midwestern Republican Senator seeks a Legislative Assistant to handle to health, labor, education and judiciary issues. This position will be responsible for monitoring pending legislation within designated policy areas; drafting and managing Appropriations Committee work in designated policy areas; drafting legislation and amendments; advising on legislative strategy; identifying opportunities for the Senator to take leadership roles; coordinating with stakeholders; preparing briefing materials; and providing support to the Senator as needed. The role requires the ability to perform effectively in a fast-paced, high-pressure environment. Ideal candidates will be self-motivated, capable of working independently, and possess strong written and verbal communication skills. Prior Capitol Hill experience and Midwestern ties are strongly preferred. Applicants should submit a cover letter, resume, and writing sample.

title: Executive Assistant
description_text: Midwest Republican Senator has an Executive Assistant position opening in the Washington, D.C. office. Qualified candidates will have one to two years of Congressional or other administrative experience and be highly organized and detail-oriented. This position provides select administrative, clerical, and office support to the Chief of Staff and Director of Scheduling. Specific responsibilities include collecting, organizing, and compiling the Senator’s nightly briefing book, coordinating and executing special events and projects, and assisting the Chief of Staff with their schedule. Applicants must be able to work well in a team environment, exercise discretion and maintain confidentiality, and have strong interpersonal, written, and oral communication skills. Proficiency working with technology and experience working with IQ or another constituent database is highly desired. This position requires an individual who can be diplomatic, remain professional at all times, exercise sound and independent judgment, prioritize, multi-task, and meet deadlines. Interested candidates should submit their resume and a cover letter.

title: Summer Press Internship
description_text: A Northeast Democratic Senator is currently accepting applications for our summer press internship in the Washington, D.C. office. The press internship gives interns the opportunity to work closely with the communications team and help support all aspects of their work. Press interns will assist with monitoring the news and drafting written materials, including press releases and social media. Interns will receive a stipend for their work. New England ties are a plus. The position is in-person in the Washington, D.C. office. The office is an equal opportunity employer; diverse candidates are encouraged to apply. We are accepting applications until Sunday, March 15, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST. The link to apply to our press internship can be found here: https://oampluspublic.senate.gov/public/login/0dc24fb4-13b6-448a-8eaf-876f7a1a141f

title: Summer Legislative Internship
description_text: A Northeast Democratic Senator is currently accepting applications for our summer legislative internship in the Washington, D.C. office. Legislative Interns will learn how the Senate functions by sitting in on committee hearings and briefings, assisting with legislative projects, attending educational events in and around Washington D.C., and leading Capitol tours. Legislative Interns will also assist with mail room duties, provide administrative support, and participate in front office staffing. Interns will receive a stipend for their work. New England ties are a plus. The position is in-person in the Washington, D.C. office. The office is an equal opportunity employer; diverse candidates are encouraged to apply. We are accepting applications until Sunday, March 15, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST. The link to apply to our legislative internship can be found here: https://oampluspublic.senate.gov/public/login/a4cdc431-36f6-4783-8b74-ab0bbf1f07aa

title: Digital Director
description_text: The Office of Senator Murphy seeks a strategic, creative, and forward-thinking Digital Director to lead and grow the Senator’s digital presence. This role will shape and execute a comprehensive digital strategy that expands the Senator’s audience, elevates his voice, and tells a compelling, cohesive story across platforms. The Digital Director leads the office’s creative team and works closely with the Senator, Communications Director, and senior staff to produce engaging content, develop partnerships with creators and new media outlets, and drive engagement in an evolving digital landscape . Key Responsibilities : o versee and execute the office’s aggressive digital strategy, ensuring a consistent and compelling presence across all platforms , l ead rapid-response digital content in a fast-moving news environment , m anage and mentor the office’s creative team, including the videographer and digital aide, providing strategic vision and direction for all digital output , c ollaborate closely with the creative team to produce a high volume of engaging content, including videos, graphics, animations, video scripts, and written copy tailored to various social media platforms , l ead production efforts, including livestreaming events; capturing and editing photos and videos; and producing frequent, high-quality video content using professional-grade equipment , b uild and maintain relationships with content creators and advise on engagement with new media and nontraditional outlets, including YouTube, Substack, podcasts, and other emerging platforms , e nsure timely delivery of high-quality content under tight deadlines, with strong attention to detail . Qualifications : m inimum 1-2 years managing a digital media program and at least 4 years of experience developing digital content , d emonstrated expertise in video production and graphic design, with advanced proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Lightroom, Premiere Pro, After Effects). Experience with photography is a plus , e xceptional written and verbal communication skills, with strong research capabilities , d eep understanding of the evolving digital media landscape, particularly nontraditional political media spaces , a bility to thrive in a fast-paced environment and meet demanding deadlines , w illingness to work evenings and weekends as needed . Candidates interested in applying for the role should submit a resume along with 3-5 samples of original digital products (i.e. graphics, video, photo). Salary commensurate with experience, range between $80,000-$90,000.

title: Staff Assistant
description_text: Senator Ted Cruz (R-TX) is seeking a Staff Assistant to join his DC office. Primary responsibilities include answering phones, processing mail, assisting with the intern program, scheduling Capitol tours, processing flag and constituent letter requests. This position manages the front office operations to include coordinating appointments and greeting visitors. Candidates must maintain a positive attitude, have strong communication and organizational skills, and be prepared to work in a fast-paced environment. Texas ties and prior Hill experience are preferred but not required. To apply, please submit a cover letter, resume, and references.
